evals.prompt: /cloud-diagram A 3-tier AWS web app with ALB, EC2 in private subnets, and RDS Aurora across two AZs
evals.expected_output: Creates a .drawio file with AWS Cloud, Region, VPC, AZ, subnet containers; ALB, EC2, Aurora icons; proper edge routing with protocol labels
expectations: Output file has .drawio extension
Contains AWS Cloud, Region, and VPC container hierarchy
Uses correct aws4 shape names (resIcon=mxgraph.aws4.*)
All edge elements contain <mxGeometry relative='1' as='geometry' /> child
Service icon dimensions are 50x50
No HTML tags in label text
Edges include protocol:port labels

evals.prompt: /cloud-diagram Azure web app with App Service, Azure SQL, and Blob Storage behind Application Gateway in a VNet
evals.expected_output: Creates a .drawio file with Subscription, Resource Group, VNet, Subnet containers; correct Azure SVG image references
expectations: Uses Azure image references (img/lib/azure2/...)
Contains VNet container with strokeWidth=4 and correct Azure styling
Contains subnet containers with dashed borders
Does not use AWS shape names
All mentioned services are present in the diagram

evals.prompt: /cloud-diagram Serverless API on AWS: API Gateway, Lambda, DynamoDB with Cognito auth
evals.expected_output: Creates a serverless architecture diagram with correct service icons and flow edges, without adding unrequested services
expectations: Does NOT add services the user did not mention (no EC2, no RDS, no VPC unless user specified)
API Gateway, Lambda, DynamoDB, and Cognito are present
Edges show request flow direction with labels
Uses correct aws4 shape names for all four services

evals.prompt: /cloud-diagram GCP data pipeline: Pub/Sub ingestion, Dataflow processing, BigQuery storage, Looker dashboards
evals.expected_output: Creates a GCP data pipeline diagram with correct gcp2 shapes and left-to-right data flow
expectations: Uses mxgraph.gcp2.* shape names
Contains Pub/Sub, Dataflow, BigQuery, and Looker
Shows data flow direction with labeled edges
Uses GCP blue colour scheme (#4285F4)

evals.prompt: /cloud-diagram png A simple AWS architecture with S3, CloudFront, and Route 53
evals.expected_output: Creates a .drawio file and attempts PNG export if draw.io CLI is available
expectations: Creates a .drawio file first
Attempts to export to PNG with --embed-diagram flag
Contains S3, CloudFront, and Route 53 with correct shapes
Does not add unnecessary VPC or subnet containers for a CDN architecture
